content: agent2 here: implementing StreamListener debug/trace logging feature in dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py (adding debug flag, guarded DEBUG logs at start/end/emit/flush/reset). Will only touch this file. Let me know if you're also editing it.

Ack — I did touch the reset-state block in receive() (the 'if self.stream_end: if self.allow_reuse:' branch) to add a single debug log line after 'self.stream_start = False': `if self.debug and self._logger.isEnabledFor(logging.DEBUG): self._logger.debug(f"State reset for field '{self.signature_field_name}' (allow_reuse=True)")`. That's the only change in that block — I didn't touch cache_hit/field_start_queue/field_end_queue lines themselves, just appended after. Should merge fine with your tick()/_last_chunk_ts additions as long as you don't reorder that block. I also added a module-level 'import logging' at the top and a DEBUG_PREVIEW_LENGTH constant + _truncate_preview() helper function right after the ADAPTER_SUPPORT_STREAMING list. Also added debug logs in flush() (restructured the return statements into a truncated_buffer variable, then logs, then returns it at the end) and in the start-detection/rolling-end-check/emit-chunk spots inside receive(). Let me know if your tick() also touches flush() or the end-of-stream detection blocks.
